title: Neo Smart Living — AI-Generated Survey Instrument
methodology: Survey questions were generated by 3 independent LLMs based on qualitative interview themes and client discovery insights. Questions appearing in 2+ model designs were prioritized. The final instrument was validated against the actual survey to confirm methodological coverage.

gpt-4.1-mini.title: Neo Smart Living — Tahoe Mini Market Assessment Survey

sections.label: Screening & Qualification
sections.purpose: Filter for homeowners with outdoor space suitable for a backyard structure

questions.text: Do you currently own a home in Southern California?
questions.rationale: Core qualification — product is targeted at SoCal homeowners
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Target Customer

questions.text: Does your property have outdoor space where a small detached structure (~117 sq ft) could be placed?
questions.rationale: Product requires backyard space; 'not sure' respondents still qualify as potential market
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Product Overview

sections.label: Category Baseline (Pre-Exposure)
sections.purpose: Measure general interest in backyard structures before showing the Tahoe Mini

questions.text: How interested are you in adding a compact factory-built backyard unit to your property in the next 2 years?
questions.rationale: Establishes baseline interest before product exposure to detect stimulus effect
questions.source_theme: The Desperate Home Office — reveals latent demand for separate spaces

sections.label: Market Demand & Purchase Feasibility
sections.purpose: Measure purchase interest and likelihood after product description exposure

questions.text: Based on the product description, how interested would you be in purchasing a Tahoe Mini at ~$23,000?
questions.rationale: Direct purchase interest at stated price point
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Research Goals — 'validate willingness to pay'

questions.text: How likely are you to purchase a Tahoe Mini within the next 24 months?
questions.rationale: Time-bounded purchase intent is more actionable than general interest
questions.source_theme: The Smart Investment Angle — property-minded buyers think in timelines

questions.text: If you were to install a Tahoe Mini, what would be its primary use?
questions.rationale: Identifies dominant use case — directly answers founder's #1 research question
questions.source_theme: All 5 interview themes map to use cases; Home Office is most frequent (n=10)

sections.label: Barriers to Purchase
sections.purpose: Quantify obstacles identified in qualitative interviews

questions.text: How much would the total cost (~$23,000) reduce your likelihood of purchasing?
questions.rationale: Cost was the most frequently mentioned barrier across all interview segments
questions.source_theme: The Budget-Practical Family — '$23K is a significant check to write'

questions.text: How much would HOA restrictions reduce your likelihood of purchasing?
questions.rationale: HOA emerged as the #1 structural barrier in founder interview and consumer interviews
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Barriers — 'HOA restrictions are our biggest barrier'

questions.text: How much would permit uncertainty reduce your likelihood of purchasing?
questions.rationale: Despite being 'permit-light,' many interviewees expressed permit anxiety
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Barriers — 'customers hear building and assume full permits'

questions.text: How much would limited backyard space reduce your likelihood of purchasing?
questions.rationale: Space constraints vary by housing type; condos and townhomes have less
questions.source_theme: Interview personas with small patios (INT01, INT04) expressed space concerns

questions.text: How much would lack of financing options reduce your likelihood of purchasing?
questions.rationale: Founder confirmed no financing exists yet; need to gauge demand for it
questions.source_theme: The Budget-Practical Family — financing is a key enabler for this segment

questions.text: How much would concerns about build quality or durability reduce your likelihood?
questions.rationale: The 'just a shed' perception is a recurring challenge from discovery interview
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Barriers — 'shed perception undersells build quality'

questions.text: How much would uncertainty about resale value reduce your likelihood?
questions.rationale: Property Maximizer segment views this as an investment decision
questions.source_theme: The Smart Investment Angle — 'comparing favorably to renovation ROI'

sections.label: Permit-Light Messaging Test
sections.purpose: Measure how permit-light positioning affects purchase likelihood

questions.text: Knowing that the Tahoe Mini is designed under 120 sq ft to minimize permit requirements, how does this affect your likelihood of purchasing?
questions.rationale: Permit-light is the product's key differentiator; need to quantify its impact
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Product Overview — 'the magic number is 117 sq ft'

sections.label: Concept Testing
sections.purpose: Compare appeal of 5 positioning concepts derived from interview themes

questions.text: Concept 1 — Home Office: 'Your commute is 30 seconds. The Tahoe Mini gives remote workers a real office — separate from the house, free from distractions.' How appealing is this concept?
questions.rationale: Home office was the most frequent interview theme (n=10)
questions.source_theme: The Desperate Home Office

questions.text: Concept 2 — Guest Suite / STR: 'Turn your backyard into a guest suite or Airbnb listing. The Tahoe Mini pays for itself.' How appealing?
questions.rationale: Guest suite has strongest ROI narrative from interviews
questions.source_theme: The Smart Investment Angle

questions.text: Concept 3 — Wellness Studio: 'Your personal wellness sanctuary — yoga, meditation, creative practice — steps from your back door.' How appealing?
questions.rationale: Wellness theme emerged strongly in interviews (n=5)
questions.source_theme: The Wellness Sanctuary Dream

questions.text: Concept 4 — Adventure Basecamp: 'Your gear deserves better than the garage. The Tahoe Mini is your basecamp — store, prep, and decompress.' How appealing?
questions.rationale: Adventure/gear storage theme captured active lifestyle segment (n=6)
questions.source_theme: The Gear Overflow Problem

questions.text: Concept 5 — Simplicity: 'One day. One price. No permits in most cases. The Tahoe Mini is the simplest way to add 117 sq ft to your life.' How appealing?
questions.rationale: Simplicity/speed messaging resonated across all segments
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Competitive Landscape — 'speed is our strongest moat'

questions.text: Which of the five concepts above is most appealing to you overall?
questions.rationale: Forced-choice reveals which positioning should lead marketing
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Research Goals — 'which use case to lead with'

sections.label: Value Drivers
sections.purpose: Measure importance of specific product features as purchase drivers

questions.text: How important is the permit-light aspect (no full building permit needed in most areas) in your purchase decision?
questions.rationale: Quantifies the permit-light USP's weight in purchase decisions
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Product Overview

questions.text: How important is same-day professional installation in your purchase decision?
questions.rationale: One-day install is a key differentiator vs. ADUs (months) and custom builds
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Competitive Landscape — 'nothing else goes from purchase to usable in one day'

questions.text: How important is build quality (steel frame, insulation, dual-pane glass) in your purchase decision?
questions.rationale: Addresses the 'just a shed' perception — quality must justify price premium
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Barriers — overcoming shed perception

sections.label: Demographics
sections.purpose: Enable segment-level cross-tabulation of all survey findings

questions.text: What is your age range?
questions.rationale: Age is a primary segmentation variable (sweet spot is 25-54)
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Target Customer

questions.text: What is your approximate annual household income?
questions.rationale: Income determines affordability; sweet spot is $75K-$200K
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Target Customer

questions.text: Which best describes your current work arrangement?
questions.rationale: Work arrangement is the strongest predictor of home office use case demand
questions.source_theme: The Desperate Home Office — remote workers are the primary segment

sections.label: Attention Check
sections.purpose: Detect inattentive responses to maintain data quality

questions.text: For quality assurance, please select 'Moderately interested' (3) for this question.
questions.rationale: Standard attention check — respondents who don't select 3 are flagged
questions.source_theme: STAMP methodology: response quality validation

questions.rationale: Founder is considering event sponsorship; need to gauge impact
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Marketing Channels — 'sponsoring trail running events'

sections.label: Permit-Light Messaging Test
sections.purpose: Measure how permit-light positioning affects purchase likelihood

questions.text: Knowing that the Tahoe Mini is designed under 120 sq ft to minimize permit requirements, how does this affect your likelihood of purchasing?
questions.rationale: Permit-light is the product's key differentiator; need to quantify its impact
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Product Overview — 'the magic number is 117 sq ft'

sections.label: HOA Navigation
sections.purpose: Understand the HOA barrier in depth — founder's #1 challenge

questions.text: Is your property governed by a Homeowners Association (HOA)?
questions.rationale: HOA status is the strongest predictor of purchase barrier severity
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Barriers — '40% of leads are in HOA communities'

questions.text: If your HOA requires approval, how would that affect your interest?
options: I would not proceed if HOA approval is needed
I would attempt the HOA process if the product came with HOA guidance
I would attempt the HOA process regardless
My HOA does not restrict backyard structures
questions.rationale: Tests whether HOA guidance materials could convert otherwise-lost leads
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Research Goals — 'how do we crack the HOA-restricted market?'

sections.label: Price Sensitivity
sections.purpose: Test price framing strategies identified in discovery interview

questions.text: When you compare $23,000 to the cost of a full ADU ($100K+), how does this price feel?
questions.rationale: Tests the 'cheaper than an ADU' price frame
questions.source_theme: Client Discovery: Pricing — 'the comparison isn't Tuff Shed at $5K, it's a contractor at $50K'

questions.text: If financing were available (e.g., $400/month for 5 years), how would this affect your likelihood of purchasing?
questions.rationale: Founder is considering a financing partnership; need to gauge demand
questions.source_theme: The Budget-Practical Family — financing is the missing enabler

sections.label: Concept Testing
sections.purpose: Compare appeal of 5 positioning concepts derived from interview themes

questions.text: Concept 1 — Home Office: 'Your commute is 30 seconds. The Tahoe Mini gives remote workers a real office — separate from the house, free from distractions.' How appealing is this concept?
questions.rationale: Home office was the most frequent interview theme (n=10)
